payload.query: For decades, political economy scholars have debated the importance of regime type for supporting foreign direct investment in the developing world. Recent scholarship, such as work by Jensen (2003) and Li and Resnick (2003), have come to very different conclusions. Considering the literature, does regime type matter for increasing FDI inflows in the developing world or are there other factors that merit scholarly attention?

articles.title: Sea Levels Are Already Higher Than Many Scientists Think, New Study Shows
